description_long: This is a detailed anatomical model of a frog, featuring a lifelike green and black mottled skin pattern with visible muscular and internal structures on one side. The model displays the frog in a crouched, alert posture, with its large, round eyes and webbed feet clearly visible. The left side of the body is partially dissected, revealing pinkish muscle tissue, a dark heart, and other internal organs, providing an educational cross-section. The skin texture appears slightly glossy, mimicking the moist appearance of a real amphibian. The model is compact, with a robust body and strong limbs, typical of a frog adapted for jumping. The overall coloration and anatomical accuracy suggest it represents a common frog species found in temperate urban parks and gardens.
description: A lifelike frog model with green and black mottled skin, partially dissected to reveal internal organs and muscles. The model is crouched with prominent eyes and webbed feet.
description_view_0: Rear-left view showing the frog's back, mottled skin, and partially exposed internal structures on the left side.
description_view_1: Left-rear view highlighting the dissected side with visible muscles, heart, and internal organs.
description_view_2: Front-left view focusing on the frog's face, large eyes, and the exposed musculature of the left limbs.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the frog's face, both eyes, and intact right side with natural skin pattern.
